issues.title: Wrong app/build under test because of mixed branches/worktrees and noncanonical launch
symptoms: The wrong LegalPDF Translate window was opened for testing.
Accepted functionality appeared to be missing because it existed only on a side branch or the wrong worktree was launched.
Repeated user-visible confusion occurred during Gemini, Gmail, and UI follow-up testing.
issues.likely_root_cause: Feature work progressed on side branches after acceptance, the approved base was not promoted immediately, and multiple runnable worktrees existed without strong enough canonical-build enforcement.

attempted_fix_history.summary: Added Qt build identity helper and noncanonical build markers.

accepted_fix.summary: Canonical build enforcement plus approved-base promotion discipline, launcher identity packet gating, and noncanonical launch override rules.
accepted_fix.expected_result: Only the canonical approved-base build is the default test target, and wrong-build launches fail closed unless explicitly overridden.

issues.title: Tests or harness cleanup reused live user state or user-facing ports and contaminated real runtime checks
symptoms: The expected localhost listener belonged to pytest or another non-user runtime instead of the visible app.
Tests reused live roaming/profile settings or bridge configuration.
The browser showed a successful handoff while the visible app stayed idle.
issues.likely_root_cause: Tests and ad hoc debugging reused live %APPDATA%, live settings, or default user-facing ports instead of isolated temp state.

attempted_fix_history.summary: Isolated pytest APPDATA and stopped bridge tests from using the live Gmail port.

accepted_fix.summary: Project guidance now requires temp env/filesystem isolation, non-live or ephemeral test ports, explicit teardown, and visible listener-conflict status.
accepted_fix.expected_result: Tests no longer hijack live machine state, and listener/runtime conflicts are triaged as harness issues before product debugging starts.

issues.title: Fragmented diagnostics across handoff, per-run execution, and finalization slowed root-cause analysis
symptoms: Browser banners, app bridge state, run reports, and finalization/draft failures had to be correlated manually.
Repeated debugging required back-and-forth between transient UI evidence and durable run artifacts.
Support packets were inconsistent across handoff, execution, and finalization failures.
issues.likely_root_cause: Project docs did not yet encode one support-packet order or one durable app-owned session-artifact pattern for multi-surface workflows.

accepted_fix.summary: Project guidance now routes multi-surface debugging through per-run artifacts first, additive workflow context when needed, one app-owned session artifact, and a fixed support-packet order.
accepted_fix.expected_result: Future host-bound workflows can be debugged from one predictable packet instead of reconstructing the incident from thread memory.
